revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Raiden_Shrine_(Isezaki,_Sakae-Iyoku)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久3581 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 47 |緯度秒 = 16.0 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 22 |経度秒 = 35.7 |祭神 = 大雷命 |神体 = |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 建保6年（1215年） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 雷電様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月25日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]の[[粕川]]沿いにある神社。主祭神は[[火雷大神|大雷命]]。[[上野国]][[佐位郡]]の上野国神明帳「於神明神」の比例社で、旧社格は郷社。
==由緒== 山城国加茂大明神より勧請したがその年月は不詳。建保6年（1215年）、赤石城主「三浦之介義澄」が鎮座式を行い創建した。 元弘三年（1333年）、新田義貞が鎌倉追討の際に社殿を修理し、戦勝祈願を行った。 永禄3年（1560年）、那波氏家臣の領主「赤石左衛門尉」が祭供料と神領を寄進した。
天正元年（1573年）1月25日、雷が境内の神木に落ち、村の修験者須田峯ノ坊が黄金像を見つけた。新田義貞の弟、[[脇屋義助]]の末裔、地頭五十久弾正が、この像を守護神と崇め、悪役災難を救済し、雷鳴の恐れがなくなったと言われた。 江戸時代に入ると、領主酒井家の崇敬が篤かった。[[寛永]]14年（1637年）は前橋潘主『[[酒井忠行]]』、[[延宝]]9年（1681年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠寛 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠寛]]』、[[明和]]4年（1767年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠温 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠温]]』が社殿を修覆した。
'''社格''' *平安時代 - 佐位郡 於神明神 従五位上（上野国神明帳） *明治4年（1871年） - 村社 *大正4年（1915年） - 神饌幣帛料供進社
==配祀== 明治40年12月5日、当社境内社や周辺の神社とその境内社を合祀 *（境内社）衣笠神社、菅原神社、八幡宮、秋葉神社 *字西馬場 飯福神社 :（境内社）神明宮、熊野神社、八幡宮、琴平神社、阿夫利神社、八坂神社、秋葉神社 *字館野 稲荷神社 :（境内社）水神社、三峯神社、八幡宮、天神社、疱瘡神社 *字左谷戸 諏訪神社 :（境内社）秋葉神社、琴平神社 *字駒形 稲荷神社 :（境内社）菅原神社、疱瘡神社 *字芝崎 飯福神社 *字若宮 稲荷神社
==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭

==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭

{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久3581 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 47 |緯度秒 = 16.0 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 22 |経度秒 = 35.7 |祭神 = 大雷命 |神体 = |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 建保6年（1215年） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 雷電様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月25日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]の[[粕川]]沿いにある神社。主祭神は[[火雷大神|大雷命]]。上野国神明帳の[[上野国]][[佐位郡]]「於神明神」の比例社。旧社格は郷社。
==由緒== 平安時代の上野国神名帳に記載されている佐位郡『於神明神』の比例社と伝えられている。建保6年（1215年）、赤石城主「三浦之介義澄」が山城国加茂大明神より勧請した。 元弘三年（1333年）、新田義貞が鎌倉追討の際に社殿を修理し、戦勝祈願を行った。 永禄3年（1560年）、那波氏家臣の領主「赤石左衛門尉」が祭供料と神領を寄進した。
天正元年（1573年）1月25日、雷が境内の神木に落ち、村の修験者須田峯ノ坊が神木に隠されていた黄金像を見つけた。新田義貞の弟『[[脇屋義助]]』の末裔、地頭五十久弾正が、この像を守護神と崇め、悪役災難を救済し、雷鳴の恐れがなくなったと言われた。 江戸時代に入ると、領主酒井家の崇敬が篤かった。[[寛永]]14年（1637年）は前橋潘主『[[酒井忠行]]』、[[延宝]]9年（1681年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠寛 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠寛]]』、[[明和]]4年（1767年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠温 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠温]]』が社殿を修覆した。
'''社格''' *平安時代 - 従五位上（上野国神名帳） *明治4年（1871年） - 村社 *大正4年（1915年） - 神饌幣帛料供進社
==配祀== [[File:Honden of Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki).jpg|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] 明治40年12月5日、当社境内社や周辺の神社とその境内社を合祀 *（境内社）衣笠神社、菅原神社、八幡宮、秋葉神社 *字西馬場 飯福神社 :（境内社）神明宮、熊野神社、八幡宮、琴平神社、阿夫利神社、八坂神社、秋葉神社 *字芝崎 飯福神社 *字館野 稲荷神社 :（境内社）水神社、三峯神社、八幡宮、天神社、疱瘡神社 *字駒形 稲荷神社 :（境内社）菅原神社、疱瘡神社 *字若宮 稲荷神社 *字左谷戸 諏訪神社 :（境内社）秋葉神社、琴平神社
==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭

{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久3581 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 47 |緯度秒 = 16.0 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 22 |経度秒 = 35.7 |祭神 = 大雷命 |神体 = |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 建保6年（1215年） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 雷電様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月25日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]の[[粕川]]沿いにある神社。主祭神は[[火雷大神|大雷命]]。上野国神明帳の[[上野国]][[佐位郡]]「於神明神」の比例社。旧社格は郷社。

天正元年（1573年）1月25日、雷が境内の神木に落ち、村の修験者須田峯ノ坊が神木に隠されていた黄金像を見つけた。新田義貞の弟『[[脇屋義助]]』の末裔、地頭五十久弾正が、この像を守護神と崇め、悪役災難を救済し、雷鳴の恐れがなくなったと言われた。 江戸時代に入ると、領主酒井家の崇敬が篤かった。[[寛永]]14年（1637年）は前橋潘主『[[酒井忠行]]』、[[延宝]]9年（1681年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠寛 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠寛]]』、[[明和]]4年（1767年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠温 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠温]]』が社殿を修覆した。
'''社格''' *平安時代 - 従五位上（上野国神名帳） *明治4年（1871年） - 村社 *大正4年（1915年） - 神饌幣帛料供進社
==合祀神社== [[File:Honden of Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki).jpg|thumb|right|270px|本殿]] 明治40年12月5日、当社境内社や周辺の神社とその境内社を合祀 *（境内社）衣笠神社、菅原神社、八幡宮、秋葉神社 *字西馬場 飯福神社 :（境内社）神明宮、熊野神社、八幡宮、琴平神社、阿夫利神社、八坂神社、秋葉神社 *字芝崎 飯福神社 *字館野 稲荷神社 :（境内社）水神社、三峯神社、八幡宮、天神社、疱瘡神社 *字駒形 稲荷神社 :（境内社）菅原神社、疱瘡神社 *字若宮 稲荷神社 *字左谷戸 諏訪神社 :（境内社）秋葉神社、琴平神社
==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭
{{デフォルトソート:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の建築物]]

[[天正]]元年（1573年）1月25日、雷が境内の神木に落ち、村の修験者須田峯ノ坊が神木に隠されていた黄金像を見つけた。新田義貞の弟『[[脇屋義助]]』の末裔、地頭五十久弾正が、この像を守護神と崇め、悪役災難を救済し、雷鳴の恐れがなくなったと言われた。 江戸時代に入ると、領主酒井家の崇敬が篤かった。[[寛永]]14年（1637年）は[[前橋藩|前橋潘主]]『[[酒井忠行]]』、[[延宝]]9年（1681年）には[[伊勢崎藩|伊勢崎藩主]]『[[酒井忠寛 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠寛]]』、[[明和]]4年（1767年）には伊勢崎藩主『[[酒井忠温 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠温]]』が社殿を修覆した。

==合祀神社== [[File:Honden of Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki).jpg|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] 明治40年12月5日、当社境内社や周辺の神社とその境内社を合祀 *（境内社）衣笠神社、菅原神社、八幡宮、秋葉神社 *字西馬場 飯福神社 :（境内社）神明宮、熊野神社、八幡宮、琴平神社、阿夫利神社、八坂神社、秋葉神社 *字芝崎 飯福神社 *字館野 稲荷神社 :（境内社）水神社、三峯神社、八幡宮、天神社、疱瘡神社 *字駒形 稲荷神社 :（境内社）菅原神社、疱瘡神社 *字若宮 稲荷神社 *字左谷戸 諏訪神社 :（境内社）秋葉神社、琴平神社
==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]境伊与久3581 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 18 |緯度秒 = 23 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 14 |経度秒 = 6 |祭神 = [[大雷命]] |神体 = |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = [[建保]]3年（[[1215年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 雷電様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月25日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Gunma }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]境伊与久にある神社。主祭神は[[火雷大神|大雷命]]{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}}。『[[上野国神名帳|上野国神明帳]]』の[[上野国]][[佐位郡]]「於神明神」の論社。旧社格は[[郷社]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>。
==由緒== [[建保]]3年（[[1215年]]）、赤石城主・三浦之介義澄が[[山城国]][[賀茂神社|加茂大明神]]より勧請したとされる。[[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）、[[新田義貞]]が鎌倉追討の際に社殿を修理し、戦勝祈願を行った。[[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]）、赤石左衛門尉・又次郎が祭供料と神領を寄進した{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}}。
『上野国神名帳』佐位郡には「従五位 於神明神」が見え、当社がそれにあたる可能性があるものの、[[尾崎喜左雄]]は大雷神社（伊勢崎市西久保）をあてている<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |last=尾崎 |author=尾崎喜左雄 |first=喜左雄 |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>。
[[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]）1月25日、雷が境内の神木に落ち、村の修験者須田峯の坊が神木に隠されていた1寸8分の黄金像を見つけた。新田義貞の弟・[[脇屋義助]]の末裔、伊与久弾正正久が、この像を守護神と崇め、悪役災難を救済し、雷鳴の恐れがなくなったと言われた{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}}。
江戸時代に入ると、領主[[酒井氏|酒井家]]の崇敬が篤かった。[[寛永]]14年（[[1637年]]）に[[前橋藩|前橋潘主]]・[[酒井忠行]]、[[延宝]]9年（[[1681年]]）に[[伊勢崎藩|伊勢崎藩主]]・[[酒井忠寛 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠寛]]、[[明和]]4年（[[1767年]]）に伊勢崎藩主・[[酒井忠温 (伊勢崎藩主)|酒井忠温]]が社殿を修理した{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}}。
'''社格''' *平安時代 - 従五位上（『上野国神名帳』）? *[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]） - 村社{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}} *[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）11月5日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}} *[[昭和]]5年（[[1930年]]）4月22日 - 郷社<ref name=":0" />
==合祀神社== [[File:Honden of Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki).jpg|thumb|right|250px|本殿]] 明治40年（1907年）12月5日、当社境内社や周辺の神社とその境内社を合祀{{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}} *（境内社）衣笠神社、菅原神社、八幡神社、秋葉神社 *字西馬場 飯福神社 **（境内社）神明神社、熊野神社、八幡神社、琴平神社、阿夫利神社、八坂神社、秋葉神社
*字館野 稲荷神社 **（境内社）水神社、三峯神社、八幡神社、天神社、疱瘡神社
*字左谷戸 諏訪神社 **（境内社）秋葉神社、琴平神社
*字駒形 稲荷神社 **（境内社）菅原神社、疱瘡神社 *字芝崎 飯福神社
*字若宮 稲荷神社 ==行事== *1月25日 新年祭 *2月25日 祈年祭 *3月25日 例大祭 *7月25日 夏祭り *10月25日 秋季例祭 {{Sfn|群馬県神職会佐波郡支部|1924|pp=63-66}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== 雷電神社古墳 == 雷電神社の社殿は、直径28メートルの[[円墳]]、雷電神社古墳の墳丘上に位置する。6世紀末から7世紀初頭の築造で、[[横穴式石室]]を有する。伊勢崎市指定史跡<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 周辺 ==
* [[十三宝塚遺跡]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |ref=harv |author=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |doi=10.11501/909821}}
== 外部リンク ==

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanhōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanhōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanhōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanhōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanhōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== Shrine Ranks === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.comment: adding religious affiliation to the ones with a broken religious affiliation section
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.

In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.

The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]
