Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>

The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}

== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>

The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.

In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular burial mound (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial mound" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District, {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}} in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. In {{ill|Genkō|ja|元弘}} 3 (1333), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}}. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the {{ill|Maebashi Domain|ja|前橋藩}} in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the {{ill|Isesaki Domain|ja|伊勢崎藩}} in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Gōsha |Established = {{ill|Kenpō (1213–1219)|ja|建保}} 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of {{ill|Isesaki|ja|伊勢崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. === types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Jūsanpōzuka Site|ja|十三宝塚遺跡}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]
