revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>

In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg|250px]] |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]

Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |prefecture = {{ill|Isesaki|qid=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|qid=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|version=101563501|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|qid=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|WD=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|WD=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]

== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|WD=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Sakaiikuyo, Isesaki)]][[ja:雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97216413]] Wikidata name: Raiden-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Riden-jinja (Sakaiiyoku, Isesaki).jpg |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Sakai Iyoku 3581 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 18 |Latitude seconds = 23 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 14 |Longitude seconds = 6 |Deities = {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Gōsha |Established = [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = March 25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Isesaki|WD=Q328596}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.30638888888889|N|139.235|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Sakai Iyoku district of [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="GunmaShrines">{{Cite book|和書 |title=佐波郡神社誌 |date=1924-12-10 |year=1924 |publisher=群馬県神職会佐波郡支部 |pages=63-66}}</ref>. It is considered one of the potential sites for the "Ojinmyōjin" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District, [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former Rank was Gōsha<ref name="Journal">{{Cite journal|和書|year=1930|date=1930-5-1|title=雷電神社昇格|journal=上毛及上毛人|issue=157|page=64|publisher=上毛郷土史研究会|doi=10.11501/3567347}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
== History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō (1213–1219)]] 3 (1215) by Yoshizumi Miura, lord of Akaishi Castle, who invited the deity from [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 3 (1333), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] repaired the shrine when seeking divine favor for his campaign against the Kamakura shogunate. In [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 3 (1560), Akaishi Saemon-no-jō and Matajirō donated offerings and sacred land<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
The {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}} for Saku District lists "Ojinmyōjin" as Ranked at Junior Fifth Rank, which may correspond to this shrine, though scholar {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} suggests another Raiden Shrine in Nishikubo, Isesaki<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |pages=342-343}}{{要登録}}</ref>.
In 1573 (Tenshō 1), lightning struck a sacred tree in the precincts. A golden statue, hidden within the tree and measuring 1.8 sun (approx. 5.5 cm), was discovered by a local ascetic. This statue became revered as a protective deity<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
During the Edo period, the shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]]. Significant repairs to the shrine were undertaken by lords of the Sakai clan, including Sakai Tadayuki of the [[:en:Maebashi Domain|Maebashi Domain]] in 1637 and Sakai Tadakane of the [[:en:Isesaki Domain|Isesaki Domain]] in 1767<ref name="GunmaShrines" />.
=== types === * Heian Period – Possibly Junior Fifth Rank (per {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}) * 1874 (Meiji 7) – Village Shrine<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1915 (Taishō 4) – Designated for imperial offerings<ref name="GunmaShrines" /> * 1930 (Shōwa 5) – Elevated to Gōsha<ref name="Journal" />
== Auxiliary Shrines == In 1907 (Meiji 40), several surrounding shrines and auxiliary shrines were consolidated<ref name="GunmaShrines" />: * (Within precincts) Kinugasa Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akiba Shrine * Iifuku Shrine, Nishibaba area (with auxiliary shrines: Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Afuri Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Akiba Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Tateno area (with auxiliary shrines: Suijin Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Suwa Shrine, Sayato area (with auxiliary shrines: Akiba Shrine, Kotohira Shrine) * Inari Shrine, Komagata area (with auxiliary shrines: Sugawara Shrine, Hōsō Shrine) * Iifuku Shrine, Shibasaki area * Inari Shrine, Wakamiya area
== Annual Events == * January 25 – New Year Festival * February 25 – Kinensai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * March 25 – Annual Grand Festival * July 25 – Summer Festival * October 25 – Autumn Grand Festival<ref>{{Cite web |title=年中行事 |url=https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/pages/6129504/event |website=郷社 伊与久雷電神社｜群馬県伊勢崎市境伊与久 |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Raiden Shrine Kofun == The shrine is located atop a circular Kofun (kofun) with a diameter of 28 meters, built between the late 6th and early 7th centuries. The mound features a stone chamber and is designated as a historic site by Isesaki City<ref>{{Cite web |title=雷電神社古墳{{!}}伊勢崎市 |url=https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/3069.html |website=www.city.isesaki.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-22 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Jūsanpōzuka Site|Jūsanpōzuka Site]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyoku-raiden.localinfo.jp/ Official Website of Iyoku Raiden Shrine]
